z
{
	{
		"using a1z26 doesn't seem to get us anywhere, but if we look at which characters are available it will give us a clue. "a" through "i" and "o", 10 characters, and we know that a=1, and i=9, we can guess that "o" is "0", this updates our decode to:

		174161018595377387932283725836301293648834223172419022725145445
		36026487402470099740341006948
		Through some trial and error, we find that these need to be shifted to base 16, then decoded as hex representation of ascii"
	}
